---
title: "City2Graph · Model public transport"
---

::: {.lead}
Turn a GTFS public-transport feed into graphs: a travel-summary graph of
stop-to-stop service and an origin-destination graph between stops.
:::

This walkthrough mirrors the
[Transportation panel](../../panels/city2graph/transportation.qmd).

## Before you begin

Import a GTFS feed first in [Import urban data](data.qmd) (**Import GTFS**). A
São Paulo feed ships under `examples/GTFS/`. The Transportation panel activates
once a feed is loaded.

## 1. Filter the service period

In **Transportation**, set **Calendar Start** and **Calendar End** to restrict
the analysis to a date range (leave as **All** for the full feed).

![Selecting the GTFS service period.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/05-calendar.png)

## 2. Travel summary graph

Press **Travel Summary Graph** to aggregate services into a stop-to-stop graph
weighted by travel time and frequency.

![A travel-summary graph between transit stops.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/05-travel-summary.png)

## 3. Origin-destination pairs

Toggle **Directed** and set **Top N** (cap the number of pairs, `0` = no limit),
then press **Get OD Pairs** to build the GTFS OD graph between stops.

![The OD graph derived from GTFS.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/05-od.png)

## Next steps

Continue with [Map an OD matrix](mobility.qmd) to bring in external flow data.
